name: Parque Independencia
address: Calle Palo Hincado, Zona Colonial, Santo Domingo Oeste
description: One of the most iconic parks in the Dominican Republic, home to the Altar de la Patria where the founding fathers of the nation rest. A symbol of Dominican identity and independence.
alt: Altar de la Patria monument at Parque Independencia

name: Acuario Nacional
address: Av. España, Sin Número, Mirador del Este, Santo Domingo Oeste
description: The National Aquarium features a stunning glass tunnel walk-through where sharks, rays, and tropical fish swim overhead. A favorite family destination showcasing Caribbean marine life.
alt: Colorful fish and coral inside the National Aquarium tunnel

name: Parque Mirador del Sur
address: Av. Cayetano Germosén, Los Restauradores, Santo Domingo Oeste
description: A sprawling urban park stretching over 7 km along the southern bluff, offering walking trails, cycling paths, and breathtaking views of the Caribbean Sea.
alt: Cyclists and walkers enjoying Parque Mirador del Sur

name: Jardín Botánico Nacional
address: Av. República de Colombia, Arroyo Manzano, Santo Domingo
description: One of the largest botanical gardens in Latin America, spanning 2 km². Explore the Japanese garden, orchid house, and thousands of tropical plant species in a peaceful natural setting.
alt: Lush tropical plants and flowers at the National Botanical Garden

name: Estadio Olímpico Félix Sánchez
address: Av. John F. Kennedy, Villa Olímpica, Santo Domingo
description: The main athletics stadium of the Dominican Republic, hosting international track and field events. Named after the country's legendary 400-meter hurdles Olympic champion.
alt: Exterior view of the Félix Sánchez Olympic Stadium

name: Centro Olímpico Juan Pablo Duarte
address: Av. John F. Kennedy, Villa Olímpica, Santo Domingo
description: A world-class multi-sport complex hosting national and international competitions. Facilities include swimming pools, indoor courts, and a velodrome — the heart of Dominican sports.
alt: Aerial view of the Juan Pablo Duarte Olympic Center facilities

name: Plaza de la Cultura Juan Pablo Duarte
address: Av. Máximo Gómez esq. Pedro Henríquez Ureña, Gazcue
description: A cultural complex housing the National Museum of History and Geography, Museum of Modern Art, National Theatre, and the National Library — the cultural soul of Santo Domingo.
alt: Plaza de la Cultura with its modernist buildings and green lawns

name: Parque Zoológico Nacional
address: Av. de los Proceres de la Independencia s/n, Arroyo Salado
description: Covering over 800 hectares, the National Zoo is among the largest in the Caribbean. Home to crocodiles, flamingos, manatees, and hundreds of endemic Caribbean species in natural habitats.
alt: Flamingos and tropical birds at the National Zoological Park
